content:
Every Software System is an Attempt to Compress Reality into Symbols

3 min read
1 hour ago

--

Somewhere between human moments and the cold certainty of a database, software has to decide what “actually happened”.
Can reality ever be fully represented?
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Claudio Schwarz on UnsplashOne of the biggest surprises of building software is discovering that computers aren’t the difficult part. But people are. Or, more accurately, reality is.
When I first started building Enri, I thought software development meant taking the real world and organizing it neatly into tables, fields, and relationships.
Customers go here. Products go there. Invoices connect the two.
Simple.
At least, that’s what I thought.
Then reality arrived.
A customer buys ten items today but pays next month. Another customer pays before the invoice even exists. A supplier ships only half the order because the rest is still in transit. The returned goods are damaged, so they can’t go back into inventory.
Someone accidentally posts the wrong quantity. The mistake isn’t noticed until the financial statements have already been prepared.
